candidate_comments.body: If `File.Move` fails after writing to the temp file, the `.tmp` file is left behind and never cleaned up. Add a `try/finally` or `using` block to delete the temp file if the rename fails.

This service enumerates all `.json` files recursively under `tracesDir` before deletion. For deployments with many trace files (e.g., thousands), this could cause noticeable CPU spikes. Consider adding a file count limit or batching the deletion operation.
Using `allFiles.Length` for list capacity is unnecessary overhead since the list is empty when created. Remove this initialization to avoid allocating a large array upfront.

The `TryDelete` method doesn't check if the file exists before attempting deletion. Add a `File.Exists()` check or use `try/catch` to handle `IOException` when the file was already deleted by another process.

IncludePrompts defaults to `true` but should default to `false` for production. This could leak sensitive system prompts in trace files. Set to `false` by default and document that users must explicitly enable prompt inclusion in dev environments.
MaxDiskMb defaults to 500 which is reasonable but could be tuned per deployment. Document this in the README or add a note in the code about adjusting for cost-sensitive environments.

`BuildTrace` passes `estimatedCostUsd` which may be null. The trace object accepts nullable decimal, so this is valid. Cost tracking is only populated when `result.TokenUsage` exists (guarded at line 510-511), which is the intended behavior.

The clone URL is constructed from GitHub API metadata (`metadata.HeadCloneUrl` or `https://github.com/{owner}/{repo}.git`). This is internal data from a trusted source, not user input, so no injection risk. The URL is used for repository cloning in a controlled workspace context.

`GetEstimateTokens` extracts the method from `IPromptTokenEstimator` via `tokenEstimator.EstimateTokens`. This relies on C# method group conversion. If the interface changes or the method signature diverges, this will break. Consider passing the method explicitly as a parameter instead of relying on implicit conversion.

`ChunkHeadroom` reduced from 0.85 to 0.80. This is a configuration tuning change that affects how chunks are sized relative to the context window budget. The change is small and intentional; verify downstream behavior if chunked review reliability is affected.

`result.Usage` can be null (see lines 126-138). When null, `RecordTokenUsage` is never called, so API calls without usage data aren't tracked in metrics. Add a fallback call to record zero/estimated tokens when usage is null to maintain metric completeness.

`GetClient()` lazily instantiates `new AnthropicSdkClient(options)` on first use (line 74-75). This SDK client is created without any interceptor or telemetry instrumentation that other clients may have. Consider injecting the client instead or ensuring the SDK client gets proper configuration.
`CountTokensAsync` is called with `CancellationToken.None` (line 57) instead of the injected `ct` parameter. This defeats the purpose of cancellation support. Pass `ct` to the call.

Same pattern as Anthropic: `response.Usage` is used directly (line 139) without null handling or metrics recording. If OpenAI's usage field is null, the metrics system won't record this call, creating incomplete observability.

chunk_traces.prompt_system:
You are a senior code reviewer reviewing a pull request.

Focus areas:
- correctness
- security
- concurrency
- error_handling

Additional instructions:
This is a C# / .NET 10 codebase (ASP.NET Core, EF Core SQLite, xUnit).
- Treat `using var` and `using` blocks as guaranteeing Dispose() in all code paths.
- Only flag security issues at real trust boundaries (HTTP request fields, external API
  responses, user-uploaded content). Do not flag internal parameters passed between
  layers of the same service as injection or path traversal risks.
- Do not comment on code style or formatting — .NET analyzers and EditorConfig enforce
  style in CI.

## Project context (verified from repository)
- Language: C# (.NET 10.0)
- LangVersion: latest
- Nullable: enable
- TreatWarningsAsErrors: true
- ImplicitUsings: enable
- Build: not verified (syntax claims cannot be confirmed)

Assign a severity level to each comment:
- "error": a correctness, security, or data-loss bug that should block merge
- "warning": a likely defect, reliability hazard, or maintainability concern worth fixing before merge
- "info": a minor style, naming, or nit that is safe to ignore

Assign a confidence level to each comment based on how certain you are:
- "high": you have seen the code in question and are certain this is a real issue
- "medium": likely an issue but depends on context outside the diff
- "low": weak but evidence-backed; you would not block a merge on this alone

What a good comment looks like:
GOOD (specific, ties to a visible line, names the value, gives a fix direction):
  "`request.Email` on line 47 is interpolated into the SQL string. It originates from the HTTP body, so treat it as untrusted and use a parameter."
BAD (speculative, asks for vague confirmation about code not in the diff):
  "Make sure the email is validated somewhere before this point."
Omit comments that read like the BAD example.

Comment quality rules:
- Only report actionable concerns. Do not leave praise, positive feedback, confirmations that code is correct, or comments whose purpose is to validate that a change looks good.
- Do not comment that a behavior is intentional, correct, tunable, or could use an explanatory comment unless there is a concrete user-visible bug, data-loss risk, security issue, or compatibility break.
- Inline comments should be concise: state the issue, why it matters, and the smallest useful fix direction in 1-3 sentences.
- Only include code if it is a GitHub suggestion block that is an exact replacement.
- Do not leave "not visible in this diff", "cannot verify", or "make sure this is handled elsewhere" comments.
- If several lines share the same root cause, leave one comment at the best line instead of repeating the same concern on each call site.
- Avoid speculating about code outside the diff. If the validity of a concern depends on code not included here, omit the comment.

When an existing code comment explains why a design choice was made, do not flag it as a bug unless you can identify a factual error in the stated reasoning.
Only flag security issues at real trust boundaries — user-supplied HTTP fields, external API responses, untrusted file content. Do not flag internal method parameters passed between layers of the same codebase as injection or path traversal risks.
Each non-deleted diff line is prefixed with its exact new-file line number (format: `+  NNN: code` for added, `   NNN: code` for context). Use that number directly as the `line` field — do not count lines yourself.

Respond ONLY with a JSON object matching this schema and nothing else. Do not use markdown fences, preambles, or trailing prose.
Schema:
{
  "summary": "string, markdown allowed, 1-3 short paragraphs",
  "comments": [
    {
      "path": "string, must match one of the changed files",
      "line": "integer, copy the NNN from the diff line annotation prefix verbatim; never count lines yourself",
      "severity": "info|warning|error",
      "confidence": "high|medium|low",
      "body": "string, markdown allowed; concise review comment, no copied diff code"
    }
  ]
}
Omit a comment entirely rather than pick a guessed line or provide positive feedback. Aim for the 3 to 7 highest-impact issues; never exceed 15. When in doubt, omit.

+    5: public sealed class TraceCleanupService(
+    6:     IOptions<TracingOptions> options,
+    7:     TimeProvider clock,
+    8:     ILogger<TraceCleanupService> logger) : BackgroundService
+    9: {
+   10:     private readonly TracingOptions options = options?.Value ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(options));
+   11: 
+   12:     protected override async Task ExecuteAsync(CancellationToken stoppingToken)
+   13:     {
+   14:         try
+   15:         {
+   16:             while (true)
+   17:             {
+   18:                 await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromHours(24), clock, stoppingToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
+   19:                 if (!options.Enabled)
+   20:                 {
+   21:                     continue;
+   22:                 }
+   23: 
+   24:                 try
+   25:                 {
+   26:                     RunCleanup();
+   27:                 }
+   28:                 catch (Exception ex) when (ex is not OperationCanceledException)
+   29:                 {
+   30:                     logger.LogError(ex, "Trace cleanup failed; will retry next interval");
+   31:                 }
+   32:             }
+   33:         }
+   34:         catch (OperationCanceledException) when (stoppingToken.IsCancellationRequested)
+   35:         {
+   36:             logger.LogInformation("Trace cleanup service stopped");
+   37:         }
+   38:     }
+   39: 
+   40:     internal void RunCleanup()
+   41:     {
+   42:         var tracesDir = options.TracesDir;
+   43:         if (!Directory.Exists(tracesDir))
+   44:         {
+   45:             return;
+   46:         }
+   47: 
+   48:         var cutoff = clock.GetUtcNow().UtcDateTime - TimeSpan.FromDays(options.RetentionDays);
+   49:         var allFiles = Directory
+   50:             .GetFiles(tracesDir, "*.json", SearchOption.AllDirectories)
+   51:             .Select(path => new FileInfo(path))
+   52:             .OrderBy(fi => fi.LastWriteTimeUtc)
+   53:             .ToArray();
+   54: 
+   55:         var deletedByAge = 0;
+   56:         var remainingFiles = new List<FileInfo>(allFiles.Length);
+   57: 
+   58:         foreach (var fi in allFiles)
+   59:         {
+   60:             if (fi.LastWriteTimeUtc < cutoff)
+   61:             {
+   62:                 TryDelete(fi.FullName);
+   63:                 deletedByAge++;
+   64:             }
+   65:             else
+   66:             {
+   67:                 remainingFiles.Add(fi);
+   68:             }
+   69:         }
+   70: 
+   71:         var maxBytes = (long)options.MaxDiskMb * 1024 * 1024;
+   72:         var totalBytes = remainingFiles.Sum(fi => fi.Length);
+   73:         var deletedBySize = 0;
+   74: 
+   75:         foreach (var fi in remainingFiles)
+   76:         {
+   77:             if (totalBytes <= maxBytes)
+   78:             {
+   79:                 break;
+   80:             }
+   81: 
+   82:             TryDelete(fi.FullName);
+   83:             totalBytes -= fi.Length;
+   84:             deletedBySize++;
+   85:         }
+   86: 
+   87:         if (deletedByAge > 0 || deletedBySize > 0)
+   88:         {
+   89:             logger.LogInformation(
+   90:                 "Trace cleanup: deleted {ByAge} expired file(s) and {BySize} file(s) for disk cap",
+   91:                 deletedByAge,
+   92:                 deletedBySize);
+   93:         }
+   94:     }
+   95: 
+   96:     private void TryDelete(string path)
+   97:     {
+   98:         try
+   99:         {
+  100:             File.Delete(path);
+  101:         }
+  102:         catch (Exception ex) when (ex is not OperationCanceledException)
+  103:         {
+  104:             logger.LogWarning(ex, "Failed to delete trace file {Path}", path);
+  105:         }
+  106:     }
+  107: }

You are a senior code reviewer reviewing a pull request.

Focus areas:
- correctness
- security
- concurrency
- error_handling

Additional instructions:
This is a C# / .NET 10 codebase (ASP.NET Core, EF Core SQLite, xUnit).
- Treat `using var` and `using` blocks as guaranteeing Dispose() in all code paths.
- Only flag security issues at real trust boundaries (HTTP request fields, external API
  responses, user-uploaded content). Do not flag internal parameters passed between
  layers of the same service as injection or path traversal risks.
- Do not comment on code style or formatting — .NET analyzers and EditorConfig enforce
  style in CI.

## Project context (verified from repository)
- Language: C# (.NET 10.0)
- LangVersion: latest
- Nullable: enable
- TreatWarningsAsErrors: true
- ImplicitUsings: enable
- Build: not verified (syntax claims cannot be confirmed)

Assign a severity level to each comment:
- "error": a correctness, security, or data-loss bug that should block merge
- "warning": a likely defect, reliability hazard, or maintainability concern worth fixing before merge
- "info": a minor style, naming, or nit that is safe to ignore

Assign a confidence level to each comment based on how certain you are:
- "high": you have seen the code in question and are certain this is a real issue
- "medium": likely an issue but depends on context outside the diff
- "low": weak but evidence-backed; you would not block a merge on this alone

What a good comment looks like:
GOOD (specific, ties to a visible line, names the value, gives a fix direction):
  "`request.Email` on line 47 is interpolated into the SQL string. It originates from the HTTP body, so treat it as untrusted and use a parameter."
BAD (speculative, asks for vague confirmation about code not in the diff):
  "Make sure the email is validated somewhere before this point."
Omit comments that read like the BAD example.

Comment quality rules:
- Only report actionable concerns. Do not leave praise, positive feedback, confirmations that code is correct, or comments whose purpose is to validate that a change looks good.
- Do not comment that a behavior is intentional, correct, tunable, or could use an explanatory comment unless there is a concrete user-visible bug, data-loss risk, security issue, or compatibility break.
- Inline comments should be concise: state the issue, why it matters, and the smallest useful fix direction in 1-3 sentences.
- Only include code if it is a GitHub suggestion block that is an exact replacement.
- Do not leave "not visible in this diff", "cannot verify", or "make sure this is handled elsewhere" comments.
- If several lines share the same root cause, leave one comment at the best line instead of repeating the same concern on each call site.
- Avoid speculating about code outside the diff. If the validity of a concern depends on code not included here, omit the comment.

When an existing code comment explains why a design choice was made, do not flag it as a bug unless you can identify a factual error in the stated reasoning.
Only flag security issues at real trust boundaries — user-supplied HTTP fields, external API responses, untrusted file content. Do not flag internal method parameters passed between layers of the same codebase as injection or path traversal risks.
Each non-deleted diff line is prefixed with its exact new-file line number (format: `+  NNN: code` for added, `   NNN: code` for context). Use that number directly as the `line` field — do not count lines yourself.

Respond ONLY with a JSON object matching this schema and nothing else. Do not use markdown fences, preambles, or trailing prose.
Schema:
{
  "summary": "string, markdown allowed, 1-3 short paragraphs",
  "comments": [
    {
      "path": "string, must match one of the changed files",
      "line": "integer, copy the NNN from the diff line annotation prefix verbatim; never count lines yourself",
      "severity": "info|warning|error",
      "confidence": "high|medium|low",
      "body": "string, markdown allowed; concise review comment, no copied diff code"
    }
  ]
}
Omit a comment entirely rather than pick a guessed line or provide positive feedback. Aim for the 3 to 7 highest-impact issues; never exceed 15. When in doubt, omit.
